claim: AI agents are non-deterministic and interactive; traditional golden-dataset testing doesn't work because correct outputs depend on runtime state
actionable: For agent testing, use simulation environments instead of static test suites; evaluate behavior in realistic, interactive contexts.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Andi Partovi: Why Every Agent Needs a Simulation Sandbox

claim: Build simulation environments for agents with: realistic users/actors with varied behaviors, tools that replicate production services, test scenarios covering edge cases, and dynamic evaluation post-run
actionable: When building production agents, create a simulation environment with realistic actors, tool mocks, and scenario generators before deployment.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Andi Partovi: Why Every Agent Needs a Simulation Sandbox

claim: Agent evaluation must be post-trace and dynamic; you can't pre-determine correct answers (e.g., agent refusing a transaction due to auth error is correct, not a failure)
actionable: Evaluate agent decisions by context, not by pre-labeled ground truth; let evaluators check traces after execution.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Andi Partovi: Why Every Agent Needs a Simulation Sandbox

claim: Agents interact with environments via POMDPs (partially observable MDPs); they take actions, get rewards/feedback, and must reason under uncertainty about system state
actionable: Design agent evaluation frameworks around POMDP principles: define actions, rewards (immediate/delayed), and test agent behavior under partial observability.
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | Andi Partovi: Why Every Agent Needs a Simulation Sandbox
